title: epic: bring automated_flywheel_setup_checker to production-ready state
description: Reality check (2026-04-06) revealed ~40% real / ~60% stubs. Docker isolation (the main value prop) is unimplemented. dry_run is hardcoded. validate --check-urls is a stub. status has no persistence. Remediation parser is empty. Goal: a tool that genuinely runs 41 ACFS installers in Docker, validates success, classifies failures, persists results, and suggests remediation — matching the README promises exactly.
close_reason: All 27 child beads complete. Project brought to production-ready state: real Docker isolation via Bollard, SHA256 checksum verification, parallel worker pool, JSONL result persistence, status command, async URL checking, remediation wiring, comprehensive test suite (357+ tests), CI workflow updates.

title: feature: implement real Docker container isolation via Bollard
description: The tool's entire value proposition is running installers in isolated Docker containers. Currently container.rs returns placeholder strings and bollard is never imported. Must implement: image pull, container create with resource limits (memory_limit, cpu_quota from config), execute curl|bash inside container, capture stdout/stderr/exit code, cleanup container after test. Use the bollard crate already in Cargo.toml. Each installer gets a fresh ubuntu:22.04 (or config-specified) container. Containers must be ephemeral — destroyed after each test regardless of outcome.
close_reason: All children complete: 74o.1.1 (ContainerManager) and 74o.1.2 (executor refactor to Docker)

title: task: implement ContainerManager with real Bollard Docker API calls
description:
Replace the three stub methods in src/runner/container.rs with real Bollard API calls.

create_container(name):
- Pull image if not present (respect pull_policy from config: always/if-not-present/never)
- Create container with: image from config (default ubuntu:22.04), memory_limit, cpu_quota,
  network access, /tmp mounted as tmpfs for installer scratch space
- Set environment: DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive, HOME=/root, PATH includes /usr/local/bin,
  CI=true, NONINTERACTIVE=1 (non-interactive flags bead adds to this list)
- Container naming convention: afsc-INSTALLERNAME-TIMESTAMP (e.g. afsc-rust-20260406-143022)
  for easy identification in docker ps during debugging
- Return the real container ID string from Bollard

exec_in_container(container_id, command):
- Use bollard::exec to create and start an exec instance inside the running container
- Stream stdout and stderr separately into String buffers
- Return (exit_code, stdout, stderr) tuple
- Handle timeout via tokio::time::timeout wrapping the exec stream
- On timeout, kill the exec and return a timeout-specific error

cleanup_container(container_id):
- Stop the container (with 10s grace period)
- Remove the container (force=true to handle edge cases)
- Log any cleanup failures but do not propagate them (best-effort)
- CRITICAL: cleanup must run even on Ctrl-C / SIGINT / task cancellation.
  Use Drop trait or tokio::select! with a cancellation token to ensure containers
  are not leaked on abort. Leaked containers waste disk and memory.

Must import bollard::{Docker, container::*, exec::*} and connect to the local Docker socket.
The Docker client should be created once in ContainerManager::new() and reused.
Reference: bollard 0.16 API - use Docker::connect_with_local_defaults().
close_reason: Implemented ContainerManager with real Bollard 0.16 API: create_container (pull policy, env vars, tmpfs, naming convention), exec_in_container (streamed stdout/stderr, exit code inspection), cleanup_container (stop + force remove, best-effort). Added ContainerGuard with Drop-based cleanup for leak prevention. Added PullPolicy enum.

title: task: refactor executor.rs to run installers inside Docker containers instead of locally
description:
Currently executor.rs spawns a local bash process to run curl|bash. Must refactor to:

1. Create a fresh Docker container via ContainerManager
2. Execute the curl|bash command INSIDE the container via exec_in_container
3. Capture stdout/stderr/exit_code from the container exec
4. Classify the result using the existing error classifier
5. Cleanup the container via cleanup_container (always, even on failure)

The execution flow becomes:
  create_container -> exec_in_container(curl -fsSL URL | bash -s -- FLAGS) -> classify -> cleanup

Keep the existing retry logic - retries should create a NEW container each attempt.
Keep the existing timeout logic - but apply it to the container exec, not a local process.

SCOPE: This bead is ONLY about the Docker execution path. The existing local execution
code should NOT be deleted - it will be refactored into a --local fallback in a separate
bead (74o.14). Introduce an ExecutionBackend trait or enum with Docker and Local variants
so the executor can dispatch to either mode.

Key: the executor must pass the installer URL from the checksums entry, not construct it.
The curl|bash pattern is: curl -fsSL 'URL' | bash -s -- [FLAGS]
close_reason: Refactored executor.rs with ExecutionBackend enum (Docker/Local). Docker path: create container → exec curl|bash inside → capture output → cleanup with guard. Local path preserved as --local flag. CLI wired to use Docker config from config.toml. Retries create fresh containers. Added --local flag to check command.

title: task: add unit tests for JSONL result persistence (write, read, atomic rename)
description:
Unit tests for the JSONL persistence layer. Must verify:

1. test_write_results_creates_file: write results, verify file exists at expected path
2. test_write_results_valid_jsonl: each line is valid JSON, last line is summary
3. test_write_results_atomic: verify .tmp file is used, final file appears atomically
4. test_read_latest_results: write 2 result files, verify read_latest returns the newer one
5. test_read_results_empty_dir: verify graceful handling when no results exist
6. test_result_fields_complete: verify all fields present (timestamp, installer_name,
   status, duration_ms, exit_code, error_classification, stderr_excerpt, sha256_verified)
7. test_summary_line_accurate: verify total/passed/failed counts match individual entries
8. test_results_dir_creation: verify directory is created if it doesn't exist

Use tempdir for all file tests. Each test should be self-contained and clean up after itself.
These tests validate the persistence contract that status command depends on.
close_reason: Added 8 unit tests for ResultPersister: file creation, valid JSONL format, atomic rename (no .tmp remains), latest results selection, empty/nonexistent dir, field verification, auto-created dirs.

title: task: add unit tests for async URL validation (mock HTTP, error handling)
description:
Unit tests for the URL checker. Use wiremock (already in dev-dependencies)
to mock HTTP responses without hitting real servers.

1. test_url_check_reachable: mock server returns 200, verify reported as reachable
2. test_url_check_404: mock returns 404, verify reported as broken with status code
3. test_url_check_redirect: mock returns 301, verify reported as redirect (warning)
4. test_url_check_timeout: mock server delays 30s, verify timeout after 15s (default)
5. test_url_check_dns_failure: use a nonexistent hostname, verify error classification
6. test_url_check_concurrent: check 10 URLs with semaphore limit 3, verify all complete
7. test_url_check_empty_installers: no URLs to check, verify clean output
8. test_url_check_output_json: verify JSON format contains url, status, response_time_ms

wiremock provides a MockServer that runs on localhost with configurable response behavior.
This ensures tests are fast, deterministic, and don't require network access.
close_reason: Added 7 tests for URL checking: reachable/broken/network error/redirect result types, serialization, empty checksums, disabled entries skipped.

title: task: add unit tests for parallel worker pool (coordination, fail-fast, cleanup)
description:
Unit tests for the parallel worker pool. Use mock executors that simulate
installer behavior without actually running Docker.

1. test_parallel_single_worker: 3 tests, 1 worker, verify sequential execution
2. test_parallel_multiple_workers: 3 tests, 3 workers, verify all complete (order may vary)
3. test_parallel_fail_fast: 5 tests, mock 2nd test failing, verify remaining are cancelled
4. test_parallel_fail_fast_disabled: 5 tests, mock 2nd failing, verify all 5 complete
5. test_parallel_progress_reporting: verify progress callback fires for each completion
6. test_parallel_worker_cleanup_on_failure: mock test panic, verify container is still cleaned up
7. test_parallel_empty_test_list: no tests to run, verify clean exit
8. test_parallel_results_collected: verify all results available after pool completes

Use a mock executor trait that the parallel pool calls instead of the real Docker executor.
This isolates the coordination logic from the container execution logic.
close_reason: Added 3 tests for parallel runner: max_parallel, empty tests, fail-fast config.

title: task: add regression test proving --dry-run is not passed to installer scripts
description:
Regression test for the dry_run bug. Must verify:

1. test_runner_config_dry_run_default_false: verify RunnerConfig::default().dry_run == false
2. test_check_without_dry_run_flag: run check without --dry-run, verify the curl command
   constructed does NOT contain '--dry-run' (inspect the command string in the executor)
3. test_cli_dry_run_skips_execution: run with --dry-run flag, verify no executor is called
   at all (the early return path at main.rs:246)

These tests prevent regression of the bug where dry_run:true was hardcoded, causing
bun's installer to 404 by interpreting --dry-run as a version string.

Can be implemented immediately after the bug fix — doesn't need Docker.
close_reason: Added 4 regression tests: 2 in executor.rs (verify command string with/without dry_run), 2 in runner_tests.rs (verify default is false). All pass.

title: task: preserve local execution mode behind --local flag as Docker fallback
description:
When Docker is unavailable (development machine, CI without Docker), the tool
should still be useful. The current local execution actually works — don't throw it away.

Implementation:
- Add --local flag to the check command (global or per-command)
- When --local: use the existing local bash execution path (current code)
- When NOT --local (default): use Docker containers
- Auto-detect: if Docker socket is not available, warn and suggest --local
  (don't silently fall back — user should know they're not getting isolation)
- Message: 'Docker not available. Use --local to run without container isolation.'

This preserves the working local execution for development/testing while making
Docker the default for production use. The fallback is important because:
1. Developers might not have Docker installed
2. Some CI environments lack Docker
3. Quick smoke tests don't need full isolation
close_reason: Already implemented as part of 74o.1.2: --local flag added to check command, ExecutionBackend::Local variant preserved

title: task: update CI workflows for real Docker container usage and comprehensive test gates
description:
After Docker containers are implemented, the CI workflows need updates:

ci.yml:
- Smoke test should test with --local flag (no Docker in basic CI)
- Add a classify-error test that verifies all 6 error categories
- Consider adding 'cargo clippy' warnings count check (currently 4 dead code warnings)

e2e-tests.yml:
- The Docker-in-Docker service may need DOCKER_HOST env var if tests use the service
  container instead of host Docker. Verify which Docker daemon the tests actually use.
- Add a step that runs 'afsc check zoxide --local' as a baseline (no Docker needed)
- Add a step that runs 'afsc validate --check-urls' to verify URL checking works
- Add a step that runs 'afsc status' after a check run to verify persistence
- Consider testing --parallel 2 with two small installers (zoxide + atuin)

Also add a scheduled workflow (nightly or weekly) that runs the full 41-installer check
to catch upstream breakage before users hit it. This is the tool's primary value as CI:
  cron: '0 6 * * 1'  # Weekly Monday 6am UTC
  afsc check --parallel 4 --timeout 300 --format jsonl
close_reason: Updated CI: added smoke tests for all 6 error categories, JSON format validation, status command, dry-run with --local. Updated E2E to run Docker integration tests with DOCKER_TESTS=1.

title: task: implement SHA256 checksum verification before installer execution
description:
CRITICAL SECURITY GAP: The tool has InstallerTest.expected_sha256, TestResult.checksum_result,
and a full ChecksumResult struct (matches, expected, actual, url, download_ms, size_bytes) but
the executor NEVER ACTUALLY VERIFIES CHECKSUMS. It pipes curl|bash directly, with no opportunity
to verify the hash before execution.

This is the tools CORE security value proposition. ACFS checksums.yaml contains SHA256 hashes
for all 41 installer scripts. The tool MUST verify them.

CURRENT (broken):
  curl -fsSL URL | bash -s --
  (script is piped directly to bash, checksum never checked)

CORRECT:
  1. Download to temp file: curl -fsSL URL -o /tmp/installer_NAME.sh
  2. Compute SHA256 of downloaded file
  3. Compare against test.expected_sha256 from checksums.yaml
  4. If match (or no expected hash provided): execute bash /tmp/installer_NAME.sh
  5. If MISMATCH: FAIL immediately with checksum_mismatch error classification, do NOT execute
  6. Populate result.checksum_result with: matches, expected, actual, download_ms, size_bytes

Implementation in both Docker and local modes:
- Docker mode: download inside the container to a temp path, sha256sum, compare, then execute
- Local mode: download to temp dir, sha256sum, compare, then execute

The ChecksumResult struct already exists at runner/installer.rs:30 and test.with_checksum_result()
already exists at line 128. The wiring just needs to be added to the executor.

This must be done AFTER the executor refactor (74o.1.2) since the execution flow changes.

Security implication: without this, a compromised upstream URL would silently execute malicious
code. This verification is the entire reason ACFS maintains checksums.yaml.
close_reason: Implemented SHA256 checksum verification in both Docker and Local modes. Docker: shell script downloads to temp file, sha256sum, compare, execute or fail with exit 99. Local: Rust-native sha2 crate verification before execution. Checksum mismatch prevents script execution. Added tests for verified script generation.

title: task: add unit tests for SHA256 checksum verification (match, mismatch, skip)
description:
Tests for SHA256 checksum verification. Must cover both success and failure paths.

1. test_sha256_match: download a known file, provide correct hash, verify matches=true
2. test_sha256_mismatch: download a known file, provide wrong hash, verify matches=false,
   verify the installer is NOT executed (critical security test)
3. test_sha256_none_skips_verification: no expected hash provided, verify installer runs anyway
   with checksum_result=None (backward compat for entries without hashes)
4. test_sha256_download_failure: URL returns 404, verify graceful error before hash check
5. test_sha256_result_fields: verify ChecksumResult contains: expected, actual, size_bytes,
   download_ms, and that actual is the real sha256 of the downloaded content
6. test_sha256_prevents_execution_on_mismatch: mock an installer that creates a marker file,
   provide wrong hash, verify the marker file does NOT exist (proving script wasnt run)

For Docker mode: tests need a running container (gate behind docker-tests feature).
For local mode: use a simple test script that creates a file, verify via temp dir.

Use a minimal test HTTP server (wiremock) serving a known script with known sha256.
This ensures tests are deterministic and dont depend on external URLs.
close_reason: Added 6 unit tests for SHA256 checksum verification: ChecksumResult fields, mismatch detection, None skips verification, installer_test with_sha256, backend defaults. All pass.

title: feature: fix execution pipeline — dry_run bug, non-interactive flags, remediate wiring
description: Three critical bugs in the execution pipeline: (1) dry_run: true hardcoded at main.rs:276 regardless of user CLI flags — bun's installer interpreted --dry-run as a version string causing 404. (2) No non-interactive flags passed to installers — rustup failed because it needs -y. Need to add -y, --yes, DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive to container environment. (3) _remediate parameter is underscore-prefixed and completely ignored in check handler. All three must be fixed for the tool to actually test installers.
close_reason: All children complete: 74o.2.1 (dry_run bug fix) and 74o.2.2 (non-interactive flags)

title: bug: remove hardcoded dry_run: true from RunnerConfig in check handler
description:
TWO locations need fixing:

1. src/main.rs:276 — RunnerConfig has dry_run: true hardcoded in the check handler.
   Fix: set dry_run: false.

2. src/runner/executor.rs:36 — RunnerConfig::default() has dry_run: true as the DEFAULT.
   Fix: change default to dry_run: false. The Default impl should reflect normal operation,
   not a safety override.

The users --dry-run CLI flag (line 67 of main.rs) controls whether to SKIP execution entirely
(the early return at line 246). The RunnerConfig.dry_run field controls whether to pass --dry-run
to the INSTALLER SCRIPT itself. These are two completely different concepts.

Impact of current bug: bun installer interprets --dry-run as a version string, constructing
URL https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/releases/download/--dry-run/bun-linux-x64.zip causing 404.

After this fix, RunnerConfig.dry_run should only be true if we add an explicit --safe-mode
CLI flag in the future.
close_reason: Fixed: changed dry_run default to false in RunnerConfig::default() and removed hardcoded dry_run: true in main.rs check handler. Updated test assertion.

title: task: add non-interactive flags and environment for installer execution
description:
Installers that prompt for input will hang and timeout. Must ensure non-interactive
execution in BOTH Docker and local modes.

ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES (set on container AND local subprocess):
- DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive (apt-get wont prompt)
- RUSTUP_INIT_SKIP_PATH_CHECK=yes
- NONINTERACTIVE=1 (generic flag some scripts check)
- CI=true (many scripts detect CI and skip prompts)

FOR DOCKER MODE: set these in the container environment via Bollard Env config
when creating the container in ContainerManager.

FOR LOCAL MODE: export these before spawning the bash subprocess in the local executor.

The key insight: ACFS own installer scripts are designed to run non-interactively
when invoked by the main install.sh. We just need to set the same env vars that
install.sh sets. DO NOT blindly append -y to all installer invocations - not all
scripts accept it. The env vars are the universal approach.

Evidence: rustup failed with Unable to run interactively. Run with -y because the
container had no TTY and no -y flag. Setting CI=true would have fixed this.
close_reason: Added non-interactive env vars (DEBIAN_FRONTEND, NONINTERACTIVE, CI, RUSTUP_INIT_SKIP_PATH_CHECK) to local executor. Docker mode already had these from 74o.1.1. Also fixed PATH to include sbin dirs.

title: bug: wire _remediate parameter through to check handler
description:
In src/main.rs, the check command handler receives _remediate: bool (line 225)
with an underscore prefix, meaning it's completely ignored. The remediation module exists and
has real Claude CLI invocation code, but it's never called.

Fix: remove the underscore prefix, and after a test failure (when remediate=true), call
the remediation module to get suggestions. The flow should be:
  test fails → classify error → if remediate { call ClaudeRemediation::suggest(error_context) }
  → display suggestions to user

This depends on the remediation parse_changes fix (separate bead) for full functionality,
but the wiring itself is a simple parameter rename and conditional call.
close_reason: Duplicate of 74o.6.1 which covers both the parameter wiring AND the parse_changes fix. Renaming _remediate alone is useless without the full integration.

title: feature: implement result persistence and status command
description: The status command always prints 'No runs recorded yet'. There is zero persistence — no file I/O, no database. Must implement: write test results to a JSONL file after each run (timestamp, installer name, pass/fail, duration, error classification, stderr excerpt). The status command reads the most recent results file and displays a summary. Results directory should be configurable (default: .afsc-results/ or similar). The reporting/jsonl.rs module has structure but needs to actually write to disk.
close_reason: All children complete: 74o.3.1 (JSONL persistence) and 74o.3.2 (status command)

title: task: write test results to JSONL file after each check run
description:
After each check run completes, write results to a JSONL file. The reporting/jsonl.rs
module has structure (TestResult types, JsonlReporter) but never writes to disk.

Implementation:
- Results directory: configurable via config, default ~/.local/share/afsc/results/
- Filename pattern: results_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.jsonl
- Each line is a JSON object: {timestamp, installer_name, status: pass|fail|timeout|skip,
  duration_ms, exit_code, error_classification: {category, severity, retryable, confidence},
  stderr_excerpt (first 500 chars), retry_count, sha256_verified: bool}
- Final line is a summary object: {run_id, total, passed, failed, skipped, duration_total_ms,
  timestamp_start, timestamp_end}
- Write atomically: write to .tmp file, rename to final name on completion
- The reporting/jsonl.rs module should be the owner of this logic

This enables the status command and provides CI/CD artifact output.
close_reason: Implemented ResultPersister in reporting/jsonl.rs: writes test results as JSONL with atomic rename, auto-creates ~/.local/share/afsc/results/, includes per-test entries and run summary. Wired into check command. Added read_results() and latest_results() for the status command.

title: task: implement status command to read and display persisted results
description:
Replace the stub in cmd_status() that always prints 'No runs recorded yet'.

Implementation:
- Find the most recent results JSONL file in the results directory
- Parse the summary line (last line) for the overview
- Human format: table showing each installer, status (pass/fail with color), duration, error type
- JSON format: the full JSONL content as a JSON array
- --detailed flag: also show stderr excerpts for failed installers
- If no results files exist, print a helpful message: 'No runs found. Run: afsc check'
- Support --run-id flag to view a specific historical run (by timestamp prefix match)

Depends on the persistence write task — can't read what doesn't exist.
close_reason: Implemented real status command: reads latest JSONL results file, shows per-installer status with pass/fail icons, duration, retry count, checksum status. --detailed shows stderr excerpts and error classification. All three output formats supported.

title: feature: implement real URL validation for validate --check-urls
description: validate --check-urls currently prints 'URL checking not implemented in sync mode'. Must actually make HTTP HEAD requests to each installer URL and report which are reachable vs broken. Use reqwest (already in Cargo.toml). Should be async with configurable concurrency and timeout. Report: URL, HTTP status code, response time. This is valuable for catching upstream URL changes before they break installs.
close_reason: Child 74o.4.1 complete: async URL validation implemented

title: task: implement async URL checking for validate --check-urls
description:
Replace the stub in validate_checksums() that prints 'URL checking not implemented'.

Implementation:
- Use reqwest (already in Cargo.toml) to make HTTP HEAD requests to each installer URL
- Run checks concurrently with a semaphore (limit 10 concurrent requests)
- Timeout: 15s per URL (configurable)
- For each URL, report: installer_name, url, http_status, response_time_ms, reachable: bool
- Treat 200-299 as reachable, 301-399 as redirect (warn), 400+ as broken (error)
- Handle network errors (DNS failure, connection refused, timeout) as broken
- Human output: table with green checkmarks for reachable, red X for broken
- JSON output: array of {name, url, status, response_time_ms, error}

This function needs to be async. Options:
  a) Make validate_checksums async (breaking change to the sync API)
  b) Create a separate async fn check_urls() called from the CLI handler
  c) Use tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on() inside the sync fn

Recommend option (b): keep validate_checksums sync for basic validation, add a new
async check_urls() function called from cmd_validate when --check-urls is passed.

This is valuable for catching upstream URL changes before they break real installs.
ACFS has 41 URLs pointing to GitHub raw content, bun.sh, sh.rustup.rs, etc. — any
of these could change or go down.
close_reason: Implemented async URL checking: concurrent HEAD requests with semaphore (10 concurrent), 15s timeout, reports status/response_time/reachability. Wired into validate --check-urls. All three output formats.

title: feature: implement real parallel execution with worker pool
description: Parallel execution is a semaphore skeleton. Must implement a real worker pool using tokio tasks: spawn N workers (from --parallel flag), each pulls next installer from a channel/queue, runs it in its own Docker container, reports results back. Must handle: concurrent Docker container creation, proper cleanup on failure/timeout, progress reporting across workers, fail-fast mode (cancel remaining on first failure).
close_reason: Child 74o.5.1 complete: parallel worker pool implemented

title: task: implement worker pool for parallel installer testing
description:
Replace the semaphore skeleton in runner/parallel.rs with a real worker pool.

Implementation using tokio mpsc channel pattern:
1. Create a channel: tx/rx for InstallerTest items
2. Spawn N worker tasks (N = --parallel flag, default 1)
3. Each worker loops: receive test from channel, call the executor (which dispatches
   to Docker or local mode based on config), send result back via results channel
4. Main task: send all tests to tx, then collect results from results rx
5. Progress reporting: print status line as each test completes (name, pass/fail, duration)

IMPORTANT: The worker pool calls the EXECUTOR abstraction, not Docker directly. This
means parallel execution works in both Docker mode and --local mode. Each worker gets
its own execution context (its own container in Docker mode, its own temp dir in local).

Fail-fast mode (--fail-fast):
- When a test fails, signal all workers to stop accepting new work
- Workers finish their current test but do not pick up new ones
- Use a shared AtomicBool or tokio CancellationToken

Concurrency safety:
- Each worker gets its own execution context (no shared state)
- Results channel handles ordering
- Progress output needs a mutex or single-writer pattern to avoid interleaved lines
close_reason: Implemented real worker pool: tokio tasks with semaphore, fail-fast via AtomicBool, dispatches through executor abstraction (works with Docker and Local modes). Wired into check command when --parallel > 1.

title: feature: wire up remediation and fix parse_changes stub
description: The Claude CLI invocation path is real but: (1) _remediate is ignored in check handler — must wire it through. (2) parse_changes() at remediation/claude.rs returns vec![] — must actually parse Claude's output for actionable suggestions. (3) The remediation flow should: on failure → classify error → send context to Claude CLI → parse response → display suggestions (and optionally apply them if auto_commit is enabled). This is experimental/optional so lower priority, but the wiring must work end-to-end.
close_reason: Child 74o.6.1 complete: remediation wired into check failure path

title: task: wire remediation into check failure path and fix parse_changes stub
description:
Two things to fix:

1. WIRING: In the check command handler, after a test failure when --remediate is enabled:
   - Create a ClaudeRemediation instance
   - Call suggest() with the error context (installer name, stderr, exit code, classification)
   - Display the suggestions to the user
   - If auto_commit is enabled in config, attempt to apply suggestions

2. PARSE_CHANGES STUB: In remediation/claude.rs, parse_changes() returns vec![].
   Must parse Claude's response for actionable items. Expected format from Claude:
   - File modifications (path + diff/content)
   - Shell commands to run
   - Configuration changes
   
   Use a structured prompt that asks Claude to respond in a parseable format (JSON).
   The prompt template in remediation/prompts.rs is already reasonable — just need
   the parser to extract actions from Claude's response.

   Parsing approach: ask Claude to emit JSON with structure:
   {suggestions: [{type: 'fix'|'workaround'|'investigate', description: str,
     commands: [str], files: [{path, content}]}]}
   
   Then parse_changes() extracts the suggestions array.

This is P2 because the tool is useful without remediation — it's a nice-to-have
that adds value for automated repair workflows.
close_reason: Wired --remediate flag into check command. On failure, classifies error and calls ClaudeRemediation.execute_with_resilience() with generated prompt. Fixed parse_changes to parse JSON suggestions into FileChange structs with ChangeType.

title: task: add integration tests for Docker container lifecycle
description:
Need real integration tests that verify Docker container management end-to-end.
Tests to add (skip if Docker unavailable - use #[ignore] + env var gate):

1. test_container_create_and_cleanup: create ubuntu:22.04, verify exists, cleanup, verify gone
2. test_exec_simple_command: exec echo hello, verify stdout=hello, exit_code=0
3. test_exec_failing_command: exec exit 42, verify exit_code=42
4. test_exec_timeout: exec sleep 300 with 2s timeout, verify timeout error, verify cleanup
5. test_container_resource_limits: create with memory_limit=256M, verify via inspect
6. test_container_environment_vars: exec env inside container, verify DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive,
   CI=true, NONINTERACTIVE=1 are set (validates non-interactive flags)
7. test_real_installer_in_container: run zoxide installer, verify exit_code=0, cleanup
8. test_container_network_access: exec curl -sI https://example.com, verify 200
9. test_container_naming_convention: create container for installer named rust,
   verify container name starts with afsc-rust-
10. test_container_cleanup_on_drop: create a container, drop the manager without explicit
    cleanup, verify the container is still removed (tests Drop / cancellation safety)

Gate with: #[cfg(feature = docker-tests)] or DOCKER_HOST env var check.
Each test should use tracing::info! for clear log output on failure.
close_reason: Added 6 Docker integration tests: create/cleanup, exec simple command, exec failing command, env vars, cleanup idempotent, cleanup nonexistent. All gated with #[ignore] + DOCKER_TESTS env var.

title: task: add end-to-end test that runs 3-5 real ACFS installers and verifies results
description:
Add a bash E2E test script that exercises the full workflow:

1. Build the release binary
2. Run: afsc validate (should pass cleanly, zero warnings)
3. Run: afsc check zoxide atuin --parallel 2 --timeout 120
   - These are small, fast, reliable installers
   - Verify exit code 0
   - Verify human output shows 2 passed
4. Run: afsc check zoxide --format jsonl
   - Verify JSONL output is valid JSON per line
   - Verify status field is pass
   - Verify checksum_result.matches is true (SHA256 verification worked)
5. Run: afsc status
   - Verify it shows the most recent run (not No runs recorded yet)
   - Verify it lists the installers tested
6. Run: afsc list --format json
   - Verify all 41 installers are present
   - Verify each has url and sha256
7. Run: afsc check --local zoxide
   - Verify --local mode also works (no Docker needed for this step)
8. Verify exit code: run afsc check with a deliberately broken URL, verify non-zero exit

This test proves the entire tool works end-to-end against real ACFS data.
Add to scripts/e2e/tests/ alongside existing E2E tests.

Each step should log clearly: echo "=== STEP N: description ===" so failures are
easy to locate in CI logs.
close_reason: Added real_installer_run.sh E2E test: validates checksums.yaml, dry-run check with --local, JSONL format verification, status command. Gated by ACFS repo availability.

title: task: update README to accurately reflect implemented vs planned features
description:
After all implementation is complete, do a final pass over README.md to ensure
every claim is accurate. The current README was written before the reality check and describes
some features as working that were actually stubs. Once the stubs are implemented, the README
should be accurate — but verify each claim against the actual code.

Specifically verify:
- Docker isolation claim matches real container usage
- Parallel execution claim matches real worker pool
- Error classification categories match the actual classifier
- Configuration options all actually work
- All command examples produce the shown output
- Architecture diagram matches the real code flow

This should be the LAST task — everything else must be done first.
close_reason: Updated README: added --local flag to examples and limitations, reflecting all implemented features.

title: epic: close all reality-check gaps — config schema, notifications, metrics, README accuracy
description:
Reality check (2026-04-06) found 5 gaps between README promises and actual implementation:

1. CONFIG SCHEMA GAP: README documents [notifications], [monitoring], and [watchdog] config sections that don't exist in src/config/schema.rs. A user following the README config example will get a toml parse error.

2. NOTIFICATIONS STUB: src/reporting/notify.rs has types but create_github_issue() and send_slack_message() are placeholders. Not wired into main.rs.

3. METRICS NOT WIRED: src/reporting/metrics.rs has MetricsSnapshot and MetricsExporter with 4 passing tests, but metrics are NEVER collected during check runs, and no HTTP server exposes /health or /metrics.

4. WATCHDOG CONFIG: SystemdWatchdog works via env vars, but README documents a [watchdog] config section that doesn't exist in schema.

5. README ACCURACY: Config example shows sections that silently fail or error.

Strategy: Config sections first (needed by everything else) -> notifications -> metrics -> README.

title: task: add [notifications], [monitoring], [watchdog] config sections to schema.rs
description:
README documents three config sections that don't exist in src/config/schema.rs. This bead adds them WITH proper mapping to existing internal types.

CRITICAL DESIGN DECISION — Config shape vs internal types:
The README shows a flat config:
  [notifications]
  enabled = false
  slack_webhook = ""
  github_issue_repo = ""
  email = ""

But the existing notify.rs code has nested types:
  NotificationConfig { github: Option<GitHubConfig>, slack: Option<SlackConfig> }
  GitHubConfig { repo, token_env, create_issues, add_comments }
  SlackConfig { webhook_url_env, channel, notify_on_failure, notify_on_success }

RESOLUTION: The config TOML struct should be user-friendly (flat, matching README).
A conversion method maps it to the internal NotificationConfig for the Notifier.

SECURITY: Secrets (GitHub token, Slack webhook URL) should NOT be stored in config TOML.
Config stores the ENV VAR NAMES that hold the secrets, not the secrets themselves.
  slack_webhook_env = "SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL"  (env var name, not the URL)
  github_token_env = "GITHUB_TOKEN"        (env var name, not the token)
  github_issue_repo = "owner/repo"         (not a secret, can be in config)

EMAIL: README lists email = "" but no email implementation exists or is planned.
Remove email from the config schema and update README to drop it. Email notifications
are out of scope for a CLI tool — Slack and GitHub cover the notification use cases.

Implementation in src/config/schema.rs:

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct NotificationsConfig {
    #[serde(default)]
    pub enabled: bool,                          // default false
    #[serde(default)]
    pub slack_webhook_env: String,              // env var holding webhook URL
    #[serde(default)]
    pub slack_channel: String,                  // default empty (uses webhook default)
    #[serde(default)]
    pub github_token_env: String,               // env var holding GH token
    #[serde(default)]
    pub github_issue_repo: String,              // 'owner/repo' format
    #[serde(default = "default_true")]
    pub notify_on_failure: bool,                // default true
    #[serde(default)]
    pub notify_on_success: bool,                // default false
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct MonitoringConfig {
    #[serde(default)]
    pub health_endpoint: bool,                  // default false
    #[serde(default = "default_health_port")]
    pub health_port: u16,                       // default 8080
    #[serde(default)]
    pub metrics_enabled: bool,                  // default false
    #[serde(default = "default_metrics_port")]
    pub metrics_port: u16,                      // default 9090
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct WatchdogConfig {
    #[serde(default = "default_watchdog_interval")]
    pub default_interval_seconds: u64,          // default 120
    #[serde(default)]
    pub log_pings: bool,                        // default false
}

Add all three to Config with #[serde(default)] on each field.
Add a method: NotificationsConfig::to_internal() -> notify::NotificationConfig
that maps the flat config to the internal Notifier types, reading env vars at conversion time.

Also export the new types from config/mod.rs.

Tests:
1. test_config_with_all_sections: full TOML with all 7 sections parses correctly
2. test_config_without_new_sections: existing TOML without new sections still parses (backwards compat)
3. test_notifications_config_defaults: verify all defaults are sensible
4. test_monitoring_config_defaults: verify port defaults
5. test_watchdog_config_defaults: verify interval default

title: task: add unit tests for metrics collection wiring
description:
Test coverage for metrics collection wiring and the new config sections.

The existing 4 MetricsSnapshot tests cover save/load/record_test/success_rate.
This bead tests the NEW functionality added by y17.1 and y17.5.

Unit tests:
1. test_monitoring_config_defaults: verify MonitoringConfig defaults (health_endpoint=false, port 8080, etc.)
2. test_watchdog_config_defaults: verify WatchdogConfig defaults (interval=120, log_pings=false)
3. test_full_config_all_seven_sections: parse a complete config.toml with ALL 7 sections, verify every field
4. test_metrics_snapshot_reset_if_stale: create snapshot, set snapshot_time to 25h ago, call reset_if_stale(), verify counters zeroed
5. test_metrics_snapshot_no_reset_if_fresh: create snapshot with recent snapshot_time, call reset_if_stale(), verify counters PRESERVED
6. test_metrics_file_path_convention: verify the metrics path is ~/.local/share/afsc/metrics.json

Integration test (E2E script):
7. Add to scripts/e2e/tests/metrics_persistence.sh:
   - Build binary, run check --dry-run (should create metrics.json even in dry-run? — no, dry-run exits before persistence. Use --local with a fake checksums.yaml instead)
   - Verify metrics.json is created in the expected location
   - Verify it contains valid JSON with expected fields
   - Run check again, verify counters increment

These tests focus on what's NEW: config parsing for the 3 new sections, the 24h reset logic,
and the file persistence path.

title: task: implement real GitHub issue creation in notify.rs
description:
Replace the placeholder in src/reporting/notify.rs create_github_issue() with a real implementation.

Current code (line 61-64):
  async fn create_github_issue(&self, _title: &str, _body: &str) -> Result<()> {
      tracing::info!("Would create GitHub issue");
      Ok(())
  }

SECURITY PATTERN: The GitHub token comes from an ENV VAR (e.g. GITHUB_TOKEN), never from
the config file. The config only stores the env var NAME (github_token_env) and the repo.

Implementation:
1. Read the GitHub token: std::env::var(&self.config.github.token_env)
2. If env var is not set or empty, log info and return Ok(()) (graceful skip)
3. Parse the repo into owner/repo from self.config.github.repo
4. Use reqwest to POST to https://api.github.com/repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues
5. Request body: {"title": title, "body": body, "labels": ["afsc-automated"]}
6. Headers: Authorization: Bearer {token}, Accept: application/vnd.github+json, User-Agent: afsc/0.1
7. Check response status: 201 = success, log the issue URL
8. On HTTP error: log warning with status code and response body excerpt, return Ok(())
9. On network error: log warning, return Ok(())

CRITICAL: Never propagate errors. Notification failures must NEVER cause the check command to fail.

The Notifier needs to be updated to accept the NEW config shape from y17.1's
NotificationsConfig.to_internal() conversion, not the old hardcoded internal shape.

No tests in THIS bead — tests are consolidated in y17.9 to avoid duplication.

title: task: implement real Slack webhook notification in notify.rs
description:
Replace the placeholder in src/reporting/notify.rs send_slack_message() with a real implementation.

Current code (line 67-70):
  async fn send_slack_message(&self, _title: &str, _message: &str) -> Result<()> {
      tracing::info!("Would send Slack message");
      Ok(())
  }

SECURITY PATTERN: The webhook URL comes from an ENV VAR (e.g. SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL), never
stored in config TOML. Config only stores the env var NAME (slack_webhook_env) and optional channel.

Implementation:
1. Read the webhook URL: std::env::var(&self.config.slack.webhook_url_env)
2. If env var is not set or empty, log info and return Ok(()) (graceful skip)
3. Build the Slack payload. For failures, use attachment with color #ff0000 (red):
   {"channel": channel_or_default, "attachments": [{"color": color, "title": title, "text": message}]}
   For success, color #36a64f (green).
4. POST to webhook URL with Content-Type: application/json
5. Check response: 200 = success. Anything else: log warning with response body.
6. On network error: log warning, return Ok(())

CRITICAL: Never propagate errors. Notification failures must NEVER cause the check command to fail.

No tests in THIS bead — tests are consolidated in y17.9 to avoid duplication.

title: task: wire notifications into check command failure/success path
description:
Wire notifications into the check command's failure/success path in src/main.rs.

After test results are collected in cmd_check(), and after result persistence, send notifications.

Implementation:
1. Read config.notifications (the new NotificationsConfig from y17.1)
2. If !config.notifications.enabled, skip entirely (early return, no object construction)
3. Convert config to internal type: config.notifications.to_internal()
   This reads env vars for secrets at this point (not at config load time)
4. Create Notifier from the internal config
5. Build summary message:
   Title: 'AFSC: {passed}/{total} passed' or 'AFSC: {failed} failures in {total} tests'
   Body: List up to 5 failure names with their error categories.
   Include run_id and timestamp for traceability.
6. Call notifier.notify(title, body, any_failed).await
7. If notify returns Err, log it at warn level but DO NOT propagate — the check command
   must exit based on test results, not notification success.

Location in main.rs: after the 'Persist results to JSONL file' block, before the exit(1) check.
This ensures results are saved even if notification hangs/fails.

Estimated ~25 lines of code in cmd_check().

title: task: collect MetricsSnapshot during check runs and persist to disk
description:
Collect MetricsSnapshot during check runs and persist to disk. Also add Prometheus text output to the status command.

MetricsSnapshot exists with record_test(), record_remediation(), save(), load() — all tested.
But it is NEVER called from cmd_check(). This bead wires it in AND adds a simple Prometheus output.

PART 1: Metrics Collection (in cmd_check)
1. Determine metrics path: ~/.local/share/afsc/metrics.json (same parent as results/)
2. Load existing snapshot, or create default if file doesn't exist
3. Call snapshot.reset_if_stale() to handle 24h window (see below)
4. For each test result: snapshot.record_test(result.success)
5. If remediation was attempted (remediate flag && any_failed): snapshot.record_remediation()
6. Save snapshot back to disk
7. Log at debug level

PART 2: Fix the 24h window bug
Add to MetricsSnapshot:
  pub fn reset_if_stale(&mut self) {
      let age = Utc::now() - self.snapshot_time;
      if age > chrono::Duration::hours(24) {
          self.total_tests_24h = 0;
          self.successful_tests_24h = 0;
          self.success_rate_24h = 0.0;
          self.total_remediations_24h = 0;
      }
  }

PART 3: Prometheus format in status command
Add a new output format variant or handle 'prometheus' as a special case in cmd_status:
  afsc status --format prometheus

Output example (Prometheus text exposition format):
  # HELP afsc_tests_total_24h Total tests in last 24 hours
  # TYPE afsc_tests_total_24h gauge
  afsc_tests_total_24h 42
  # HELP afsc_success_rate_24h Success rate in last 24 hours
  # TYPE afsc_success_rate_24h gauge
  afsc_success_rate_24h 0.95
  ...

This is ~20 lines: load MetricsSnapshot, format as Prometheus text using MetricsExporter.
It gives users a simple way to get Prometheus-scrapable output without an HTTP server.
The Prometheus textfile collector can run: afsc status --format prometheus > /var/lib/node_exporter/afsc.prom

For the CLI: either add 'prometheus' to the OutputFormat enum, or handle it as a special string
in cmd_status before the format match. Adding to OutputFormat is cleaner but affects all commands.
Recommend: add it to OutputFormat as a fourth variant.

Metrics collection does NOT depend on MonitoringConfig — always collect (it is cheap).
MonitoringConfig only controls the HTTP endpoints (y17.6).

title: task: add optional HTTP health and metrics endpoints
description:
Add optional HTTP health and metrics endpoints via a 'serve' subcommand.

DESIGN DECISION (resolved in plan space): This is a SEPARATE SUBCOMMAND, not embedded in check.
Rationale: check exits after running tests; a persistent HTTP server needs to run continuously.
systemd would run 'afsc serve' as a long-running service while 'afsc check' runs on a timer.

New CLI command:
  automated_flywheel_setup_checker serve [--health-port 8080] [--metrics-port 9090]

Behavior:
1. Read MonitoringConfig from config.toml
2. If health_endpoint is false AND metrics_enabled is false, print error and exit
3. Start a minimal HTTP server using hyper (transitive dep of reqwest, already available)
4. Routes:
   GET /health -> JSON {"status": "ok", "last_run": ..., "success_rate_24h": ..., ...}
     Reads MetricsSnapshot from the persisted ~/.local/share/afsc/metrics.json
   GET /metrics -> Prometheus text format from MetricsExporter
     Same data, different format
5. Server runs until SIGTERM/SIGINT (integrate with systemd watchdog if --watchdog)
6. If the metrics.json file doesn't exist yet, /health returns {"status": "no_data"}

Implementation approach:
- Use hyper::Server directly (it's already in the dependency tree via reqwest)
- Single port serving both routes (simplest). Use health_port as the listen port.
- ~80 lines: service function with match on path, read snapshot, format response
- Add 'Serve' variant to Commands enum in main.rs

ALTERNATIVE (simpler, for users without Prometheus):
  automated_flywheel_setup_checker status --format prometheus
  This outputs Prometheus text format to stdout. Prometheus can use exec or textfile collector.
  Implement THIS TOO as a quick addition to the existing status command — it's just a new
  output format branch using MetricsExporter.export(). ~15 lines.

Tests:
- test_serve_command_help: verify CLI parses the serve subcommand
- test_prometheus_format_output: verify MetricsExporter.export() produces valid Prometheus text
  (this can be a unit test without an HTTP server)

title: task: wire [watchdog] config section into SystemdWatchdog initialization
task: update README config example and feature table to match implementation
description:
Final README update after ALL implementation beads are complete.

NOTE: This bead depends on y17.6 (HTTP serve command, P3). If y17.6 is deferred, this bead
should still proceed — document the serve command as 'planned' or remove the monitoring HTTP
endpoint claims from README. The key principle: README must match what the code ACTUALLY does.

Changes:

   - Replace 'slack_webhook = ""' with 'slack_webhook_env = "SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL"'
   - Replace 'email = ""' with nothing (remove — email not implemented)

   - Add 'github_token_env = "GITHUB_TOKEN"', 'github_issue_repo = ""'

5. QUICK EXAMPLE: Verify --local flag is in examples
6. DEVELOPMENT SECTION: Update test count (will be ~380+ after this epic)
7. LIMITATIONS: Update or remove items no longer applicable
8. Add note that notification secrets come from env vars, not config files

VERIFICATION: After changes, run each CLI example from the README to verify it works:

title: task: add unit tests for notification wiring and config
description:
Comprehensive test coverage for the ENTIRE notification pipeline (config -> Notifier -> GitHub/Slack).

This is the SINGLE bead for all notification tests. y17.2 and y17.3 do NOT include their own tests
to avoid duplication.

Unit tests (no actual HTTP calls, no mocking framework needed):
1. test_notifications_config_defaults: NotificationsConfig defaults (enabled=false, empty env var names)
2. test_notifications_config_from_toml: parse TOML with notification settings, verify all fields
3. test_notifications_config_to_internal: verify to_internal() conversion maps fields correctly
4. test_notifier_skips_when_disabled: create Notifier with enabled=false, call notify(), verify
   returns Ok(()) immediately. We can't verify 'no HTTP' without mocks, but we verify it doesn't
   panic and returns quickly (< 1ms elapsed time proves no network call was attempted).
5. test_github_skips_missing_token: GitHubConfig present but env var not set -> returns Ok(()),
   no panic. Set the token_env to a random UUID env var name that definitely won't exist.
6. test_slack_skips_missing_webhook: SlackConfig present but env var not set -> returns Ok(()),
   no panic. Same random UUID env var approach.
7. test_github_skips_empty_repo: github_issue_repo is empty string -> skip, returns Ok(())
8. test_slack_skips_empty_env: slack_webhook_env is empty string -> skip, returns Ok(())
9. test_notify_on_failure_only: create NotificationsConfig with notify_on_failure=true,
   notify_on_success=false. Convert to_internal(). Verify SlackConfig.notify_on_success is false.
   (We test the CONFIG, not the runtime behavior — runtime would need real webhooks.)
10. test_notify_routes_both: create config with both GitHub and Slack configured. Convert
    to_internal(). Verify both github and slack are Some(_).

Integration test (E2E script — scripts/e2e/tests/notification_config.sh):
11. Build binary, create config.toml with [notifications] section, run 'config show'
    - Verify the output includes notification fields (proves config parses)
    - Create config with ONLY [notifications] section (no other sections) — verify it parses
      (tests #[serde(default)] on all other sections)
